user_message: Just got this from Steven Wilson at Tailspin Toys -- I need help figuring out pipeline hygiene. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: ECIF or partner engagement needed for migration

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF17-D trap (Migration sequencing ignores dependencies); produces complete Architecture sketch with service recommendations (justified), migration sequencing, dependency map,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF17-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF17-D failure mode (Migration sequencing ignores dependencies)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF17-D -- Migration sequencing ignores dependencies
